Title: Two nucleotide sugar transporters are important for cell wall integrity and full virulence of Magnaporthe oryzae

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nucleotide sugar transporter; triosephosphate transporter (TPT) family domain; transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: maintains fungal cell wall integrity, appressorium maturation/adhesion, immune evasion, and virulence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in mature appressoria (12 hpi)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in mature appressoria (12 hpi)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; ER colocalization with RFP-HDEL; heterologous yeast sugar transport assay; GC-MS of soluble cell wall sugars; virulence assay; staining assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: different sugar supplies; cell wall stress agents (Congo red, Calcofluor white, SDS); H2O2; DPI treatment during infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation/utilization of mannose- and glucose-related cell wall saccharides, maintains cell wall integrity and glycoprotein shielding, and is required for full virulence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST1 encodes an ER-localized nucleotide sugar transporter required for transport of mannose and glucose in yeast complementation assays. 2. Deletion of NST1 reduced soluble cell wall sugars, impaired cell wall integrity, growth, conidiation, appressorial maturation, glycogen utilization, and virulence. 3. Loss of NST1 reduced outer cell wall glycoproteins and increased chitin exposure, enhancing host ROS/defence responses.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes reduced vegetative growth, conidiation, appressorial turgor/adhesion, invasive growth, and increased sensitivity to ROS/cell wall stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it functions as a transporter rather than enzyme or transcriptional regulator; discussion notes some possible effects on fructose/sucrose-related profiles, but strongest direct transport evidence supports mannose and glucose

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nucleotide sugar transporter; triosephosphate transporter (TPT) family domain; transmembrane domain
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: maintains fungal cell wall integrity, appressorium maturation/adhesion, immune evasion, and virulence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in mature appressoria (12 hpi) and early invasive hyphae (18-24 hpi)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in mature appressoria (12 hpi) and early invasive hyphae (18-24 hpi)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; complementation; ER colocalization with RFP-HDEL; heterologous yeast sugar transport assay; GC-MS of soluble cell wall sugars; virulence assay; staining assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: different sugar supplies; cell wall stress agents (Congo red, Calcofluor white, SDS); H2O2; DPI treatment during infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes mannose-related cell wall saccharide accumulation and cell wall integrity, and is required for full virulence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST2 encodes an ER-localized nucleotide sugar transporter with substrate preference for mannose in yeast complementation assays. 2. Deletion of NST2 altered soluble cell wall sugar content, impaired cell wall integrity, growth, conidial morphology, conidiation, appressorial maturation, and virulence. 3. Loss of NST2 reduced outer cell wall glycoproteins and increased chitin exposure, enhancing host defence activation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes reduced vegetative growth, altered conidial morphology, reduced conidiation, appressorial defects, invasive growth defects, and increased sensitivity to ROS/cell wall stress
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it functions as a transporter rather than enzyme or transcriptional regulator; strongest direct transport evidence supports mannose only
